examples: Good communication skills are essential in this role.
I think sleep is essential if you want to stay focused.

conversation.question: Is sleep really that important for studying?
conversation.answer: Yes, sleep is essential if you want to stay focused. Even one bad night can hurt your memory.

meaningEn: to delay doing something until a later time
examples: Don't put off your homework until Sunday.
We had to put off the meeting because of a client issue.
I keep putting off calling the bank, but I should do it today.

conversation.question: Did you call the bank yet?
conversation.answer: Not yet. I keep putting it off, but I should do it today.

meaningEn: used to introduce a new subject or add information
examples: By the way, did you finish the report?
I'll send the file now. By the way, the deadline moved to Friday.
Nice to see you. By the way, I liked your presentation yesterday.

conversation.question: Thanks for sending the file.
conversation.answer: No problem. By the way, the deadline moved to Friday, so we still have time.

meaningEn: to choose something after thinking about it
examples: I need to make a decision by tonight.
Our manager made a decision to hire two more developers.
It's hard to make a decision when we don't have enough data.

conversation.question: We don't have enough data yet.
conversation.answer: I know, but we still need to make a decision by tonight. Let's use what we have.

meaningEn: to feel pleased about something that is going to happen
examples: I look forward to the weekend.
We look forward to working with your team on this project.
I'm looking forward to hearing your thoughts on the proposal.

conversation.question: Are you excited about the new project?
conversation.answer: Yes, I look forward to working with your team. I think it'll be a great experience.
